title: helictotrichon gonzaloi
intro: Helictotrichon gonzaloi, also known as Gonzalo's false brome, is a species of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. This perennial grass is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Helictotrichon gonzaloi include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reduced inflammation and pain. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
